meta.definition: syntax_valid AND schema_valid BUT security_score < threshold

interpretation.key_finding: 7.1% of deployable IaC (syntax+schema valid) has poor security, creating a 'silent danger' that syntax-only checkers cannot detect.
interpretation.implication: Deploying based on syntax/schema validity alone would expose 2 tasks worth of insecure infrastructure.

taxonomy_table.reason: Syntax errors are highly local and precise; parsers give exact line numbers

taxonomy_table.description: Valid syntax but wrong structure (missing required fields, wrong types)
taxonomy_table.reason: Schema errors name the field but often don't specify valid values; models may misapply the fix

taxonomy_table.description: Insecure configurations caught by policy checkers
taxonomy_table.reason: Security checks identify problems but rarely say what secure value to use; some fixes require architectural changes

taxonomy_table.description: Errors involving relationships between multiple resources
taxonomy_table.reason: Cross-resource errors require understanding the whole system topology; fixing one resource may require coordinated changes across many

expert.note: No condition achieves 70% for expert difficulty

medium.recommendation: Human expert review required before deployment

hard.recommendation: Human expert review required before deployment

expert.recommendation: Human expert review required before deployment

decision_tree.rationale: Easy tasks with low risk can be handled autonomously with >70% success rate
For high-risk environments, automated SC adds security coverage for easy tasks
Medium tasks benefit from SC to boost performance above the 70% threshold
High-risk medium tasks need human oversight to ensure security compliance
Hard/expert tasks fall below acceptable thresholds for AI-only or AI+SC
Expert tasks with compliance requirements need full human expertise

interpretation.key_finding: Checkov and Trivy agree 84.9% of the time (kappa=0.368, fair agreement). The two tools are complementary for security assessment.
